From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Squash championship held in Hopton-on-Sea, Great Yarmouth, Norfolk

| 2018 World Indoor [Bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") Championship | |
| --- | --- |
| 40th World Indoor Bowls Championship | |
| Location | [England](/wiki/England "England") [Hopton-on-Sea](/wiki/Hopton-on-Sea "Hopton-on-Sea"), [Great Yarmouth](/wiki/Great_Yarmouth "Great Yarmouth"), [Norfolk](/wiki/Norfolk "Norfolk") |
| Date | 12–28 January 2018 |
| Category | [World Indoor Championships](/wiki/World_Indoor_Bowls_Championships "World Indoor Bowls Championships") |
| ← [2017](/wiki/2017_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2017 World Indoor Bowls Championship")  [2019](/wiki/2019_World_Indoor_Bowls_Championship "2019 World Indoor Bowls Championship") → | |

The **2018 Just World Indoor Bowls Championship** took place at [Potters Leisure Resort](/wiki/Potters_Resort "Potters Resort"), [Hopton-on-Sea](/wiki/Hopton-on-Sea "Hopton-on-Sea"), [Great Yarmouth](/wiki/Great_Yarmouth "Great Yarmouth"), [England](/wiki/England "England"), on 12–28 January 2018.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

[Mark Dawes](/wiki/Mark_Dawes "Mark Dawes") and [Robert Paxton](/wiki/Robert_Paxton_(bowls) "Robert Paxton (bowls)") reached the Open singles final with Dawes taking the title on a tie break. Dawes had been the player of the tournament.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
[Katherine Rednall](/wiki/Katherine_Rednall "Katherine Rednall") won her third singles title defeating [Rebecca Field](/wiki/Rebecca_Field_(bowls) "Rebecca Field (bowls)") in the final.[[3]](#cite_note-3)
The seventh seeds [Mark Dawes](/wiki/Mark_Dawes "Mark Dawes") and [Jamie Chestney](/wiki/Jamie_Chestney "Jamie Chestney") won the Open pairs final beating [Nick Brett](/wiki/Nick_Brett "Nick Brett") and [Greg Harlow](/wiki/Greg_Harlow "Greg Harlow"). Chestney missed the opportunity to win the first set with his last bowl but gained a three count to draw the set 6–6; the second set was won 9–5, helped by a decisive 5–0 count on end five.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Chestney also won the Mixed Pairs title with [Lesley Doig](/wiki/Lesley_Doig "Lesley Doig").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Northern Ireland's [Chloe Watson](/wiki/Chloe_Watson "Chloe Watson") received the Open Under-25 Singles title after her opponent, defending champion [Ellen Ryan](/wiki/Ellen_Ryan_(bowls) "Ellen Ryan (bowls)"), was unable to complete the final as she felt unwell.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)
